keen.linePrev: And somewhere out there in the stars
keen.line: A keen-eyed lookout spied a flickering light
stanza: We watched the tragedy unfold
We did as we were told, bought and sold
It was the greatest show on Earth
But then, it was over
We oohed and ahhed, we drove our racing cars
We ate our last few jars of caviar
And somewhere out there in the stars
A keen-eyed lookout spied a flickering light
(Our last hurrah)
Our last hurrah
And when they found our shadows
Grouped 'round the TV sets
They ran down every lead
They repeated every test
They checked out all the data on their list
And then, the alien anthropologists
Admitted they were still perplexed
But on eliminating every other reason for our sad demise
They logged the only explanation left
This species has amused itself to death
(Amused itself to death, amused itself to death)
(Amused itself to death, amused itself to death)

keen.lineNext: Ululate the lost Great American Novels —
stanza: And as for my inflammatory writ?
Well, I wrote it and I was not inflamed one bit
Advice from the master derailed that disaster;
He said "Hand that pen over to me, poetaster!"
While across the great plains
Keening lovely & awful
Ululate the lost Great American Novels —
An unlawful lot, left to stutter and freeze, floodlit
(But at least they didn't run
To their undying credit.)

krishna.line: Ol' Hare Krishna got nothing on you
krishna.lineNext: Just keep you crazy with nothing to do
stanza: Ol' Hare Krishna got nothing on you
Just keep you crazy with nothing to do
Keep you occupied with pie in the sky
There ain't no guru who can see through your eyes

karma's.lineNext: Gonna knock you right in the head
stanza: Instant karma's gonna get you
Gonna knock you right in the head
You better get yourself together
Pretty soon, you're gonna be dead
What in the world you thinkin' of
Laughin' in the face of love?
What on earth you tryin' to do?
It's up to you, yeah, you

korean.linePrev: And Joe is somewhere on the road
korean.line: We lost Davy in the Korean War
korean.lineNext: And I still don't know what for, don't matter anymore
stanza: We had an apartment in the city
Me and Loretta liked living there
Well, it'd been years since the kids had grown
A life of their own left us alone
John and Linda live in Omaha
And Joe is somewhere on the road
We lost Davy in the Korean War
And I still don't know what for, don't matter anymore

kentucky.line: I know a puppy who walked from Kentucky
kentucky.lineNext: Made it to East Virginia by dawn
stanza: I know a puppy who walked from Kentucky
Made it to East Virginia by dawn
He had seventeen ideas in his head

kentucky.linePrev: Out of writing sad songs and gettin' paid by the tear
stanza: Marry me and leave Kentucky
Come to Tennessee
'Cause you're the only ten I see
You're the only ten I see

kentucky.linePrev: The city can put a hurtin' on you
kentucky.line: Especially a fella from eastern Kentucky
kentucky.lineNext: Without a penny or a worth to his name
stanza: I was out of my mind the first night we met
I was feeling lonely and blue
I'd just moved to the country to get me some rest
The city can put a hurtin' on you
Especially a fella from eastern Kentucky
Without a penny or a worth to his name
Wasting his life on a burnin' desire
And chasing that honky tonk flame

kentucky.line: She met a boy up in Kentucky
stanza: She met a boy up in Kentucky
Charlie was his name
Just when he thought he got lucky
She stole his watch and chain

partner.line: Just when he thought he got lucky

kentucky.linePrev: Good enough for me and my Bobby McGee.
kentucky.line: From the coalmines of Kentucky to the California sun
kentucky.lineNext: Bobby shared the secrets of my soul
stanza: From the coalmines of Kentucky to the California sun
Bobby shared the secrets of my soul
Standin' right beside me, Lord, through everythin' I done
Every night she kept me from the cold

kentucky.songTitle: My Old Kentucky Home, Good Night
kentucky.linePrev: We will sing one song for the old Kentucky home
kentucky.line: For the old Kentucky home far away
kentucky.lineNext: They hunt no more for the 'possum and the coon
stanza: Weep no more my lady, oh! weep no more today!
We will sing one song for the old Kentucky home
For the old Kentucky home far away

kentucky.songTitle: My Old Kentucky Home, Good Night
kentucky.linePrev: By 'n by hard times comes a-knocking at the door
kentucky.line: Then my old Kentucky home, good night
kentucky.lineNext: Weep no more my lady, oh! weep no more today!
stanza: The sun shines bright in the old Kentucky home
'Tis summer, the darkies are gay
The corn top's ripe and the meadow's in the bloom
While the birds make music all the day
The young folks roll on the little cabin floor
All merry, all happy, and bright
By 'n by hard times comes a-knocking at the door
Then my old Kentucky home, good night

kentucky.songTitle: My Old Kentucky Home, Good Night
kentucky.linePrev: The time has come when the darkies have to part
kentucky.line: Then my old Kentucky home, good night!
kentucky.lineNext: Weep no more my lady, oh! weep no more today!
stanza: They hunt no more for the 'possum and the coon
On the meadow, the hill and the shore
They sing no more by the glimmer of the moon
On the bench by the old cabin door
The day goes by like a shadow o'er the heart
With sorrow where all was delight
The time has come when the darkies have to part
Then my old Kentucky home, good night!

kentucky.songTitle: My Old Kentucky Home, Good Night
kentucky.linePrev: A few more days till we totter on the road
kentucky.line: Then my old Kentucky home, good-night!
kentucky.lineNext: Weep no more my lady, oh! weep no more today!
stanza: The head must bow and the back will have to bend
Wherever the darkies may go
A few more days and the trouble all will end
In the field where the sugar-canes grow
A few more days for to tote the weary load
No matter 'twill never be light
A few more days till we totter on the road
Then my old Kentucky home, good-night!

knockin.line: I'm knockin' on your screen door in the summertime
knockin.lineNext: Everybody's out there climbin' on the trees now

I can hear the train tracks through the laundry on the line
I'm thinking it's your business, but you don't got to answer
I'm knockin' on your screen door in the summertime

knockin.line: Bad news comes knockin' at your garden gate
stanza: Everythin' comes and goes
Pleasure moves on too early and trouble leaves too slow
Just when you're thinkin' you've finally got it made
Bad news comes knockin' at your garden gate
Then I go to my brother
And I say, "Brother, help me please"
But he winds up
Knockin' me
Back down on my knees
Lor', there been times that I thought
I couldn't last for long
But now, I think I'm able to
Carry on

knockin.linePrev: Bad news comes knockin' at your garden gate
knockin.lineNext: You're a brute, you're an angel, you can crawl, you can fly too

You're a brute, you're an angel, you can crawl, you can fly too
It's down to you
It all comes down to you

keyhole.line: And peeking through keyholes in numbered doors
keyhole.lineNext: Where the players lick their wounds
stanza: I looked a coyote right in the face
On the road to Baljennie near my old hometown
He went running through the whisker wheat
Chasing some prize down
And a hawk was playing with him
Coyote was jumping straight up and making passes
He had those same eyes just like yours
Under your dark glasses
Privately probing the public rooms
And peeking through keyholes in numbered doors
Where the players lick their wounds
And take their temporary lovers
And their pills and powders to get them through this passion play
No regrets, Coyote
I just get off up aways

kindly.lineNext: So this is how I hide the hurt
stanza: I wish that he was here tonight
It's so hard to obey
His sad request of me
To kindly stay away
So this is how I hide the hurt

kindly.linePrev: For somethin' wet and cold as that old cell
kindly.line: That kindly jailer grinned at me

Then poured himself another beer
And came and whispered in my ear
'If booze was just a dime a bottle
Boy, you couldn't even buy the smell'

kindly.linePrev: And as those years go by they'll look upon you

Your branch among the wood
You'll try to tell them, but you're lost for words
'Cause it's so absurd, how good
And as those years go by they'll look upon you
Kindly like a friend
A pattern in repeat
And never ends

klieg.lineNext: But the lights are out on the mean streets
stanza: Outside it's a bright night, there's an opera at Lincoln Center
Movie stars arrive by limousine
The klieg lights shoot up over the skyline of Manhattan
But the lights are out on the mean streets

kemosabe.lineNext: Kiss my ass, I bought a boat, I'm going out to sea"
stanza: Now the mystery masked man was smart
He got himself a Tonto
'Cause Tonto did the dirty work for free
But Tonto he was smarter
And one day said, "Kemosabe
Kiss my ass, I bought a boat, I'm going out to sea"

kathy's.linePrev: Sometimes I think that boy, he ain't got no ears
kathy's.line: And Kathy's laughing, Kathy is always laughing
kathy's.lineNext: Well keep on laughing, Kathy, just make it out of here

Sometimes I think that boy, he ain't got no ears
And Kathy's laughing, Kathy is always laughing
Well keep on laughing, Kathy, just make it out of here

kathy.linePrev: And we walked off to look for America
kathy.line: Kathy, I said, as we boarded a Greyhound in Pittsburgh
kathy.lineNext: Michigan seems like a dream to me now
stanza: Kathy, I said, as we boarded a Greyhound in Pittsburgh
Michigan seems like a dream to me now
It took me four days to hitchhike from Saginaw

kathy.linePrev: And the moon rose over an open field
kathy.line: Kathy, I'm lost, I said, though I knew she was sleeping
kathy.lineNext: I'm empty and I'm aching and I don't know why
stanza: Kathy, I'm lost, I said, though I knew she was sleeping
I'm empty and I'm aching and I don't know why
Counting the cars on the New Jersey turnpike

kroc.linePrev: And I gotta see a good thing sure enough now
kroc.line: Oh, my name's not Crock, it's Kroc with a K
kroc.lineNext: Like crocodile but not spelt that way now
stanza: Oh, my name's not Crock, it's Kroc with a K
Like crocodile but not spelt that way now
It's dog eat dog, rat eat rat
Kroc style, boom like that

kroc.linePrev: Every little thing gotta stay the same
kroc.line: Oh, my name's not Crock, that's Kroc with a K
kroc.lineNext: Like crocodile but not spelt that way now
stanza: Oh, my name's not Crock, that's Kroc with a K
Like crocodile but not spelt that way now
It's dog eat dog, rat eat rat
Dog eat dog, rat eat rat, now
Oh, it's dog eat dog, rat eat rat
Kroc style, boom like that

kroc.linePrev: It's dog eat dog, rat eat rat
kroc.lineNext: And folks line up all down the street
stanza: Oh, my name's not Crock, it's Kroc with a K
Like crocodile but not spelt that way now
It's dog eat dog, rat eat rat
Kroc style, boom like that
Alone in her cell
No dope in her veins
The killer'd become
Little girl lost again
She fell to her knees
She prayed she would die
On the cold cement floor
She finally cried

karla.linePrev: Her last words were "I love you all,"
karla.lineNext: Now it's an eye for an eye
stanza: And love came like the wind
Love whispered her name
It reached through and held her
Lifted her pain
15 years on death row
Her faith deeper each day
Her last words were "I love you all,"
Good-bye, Karla Faye

kate.linePrev: Dandy Dave, Rusty Nails and Sweet Lady Sugar Cane
kate.line: Dead Eye Kate and the Baloney Kid raise their cups tonight in Steam Train's name
kate.lineNext: Senators, congressmen, puppets on a string
stanza: 2 ContributorsLast of the Hobo Kings LyricsSteam Train Maury died last night
His wife Wanda by his side
He caught the Westbound out of here
Hopped the high irons to the by and by
They say he jumped ten thousand trains
Rode a million miles for free
Helped out at VA hospitals
And penitentiaries
Dandy Dave, Rusty Nails and Sweet Lady Sugar Cane
Dead Eye Kate and the Baloney Kid raise their cups tonight in Steam Train's name
Senators, congressmen, puppets on a string
Among the windswept vagabonds Steam Train was the king
The last of the hobo kings
The last of the hobo kings

krewe.linePrev: And roll on, brother, in the wheel inside the wheel
krewe.line: The Krewe of the Crossbones parades into the midnight sun
krewe.lineNext: They march through the fire and come out beating homemade drums
stanza: The Krewe of the Crossbones parades into the midnight sun
They march through the fire and come out beating homemade drums
While the French Quarter Queens in their high-heeled drag disguise
Sing "Over the Rainbow" 'til Judy Garland quivers and sighs

kindle.linePrev: 37 ContributorsRavens Lyrics“Ravens” is the third song from A Crow Looked at Me…
kindle.line: I'd just finished splitting up the scrap two-by-fours into kindling
kindle.lineNext: I glanced up at the half moon, pink, chill refinery cloud light
stanza: 37 ContributorsRavens Lyrics“Ravens” is the third song from A Crow Looked at Me…
I'd just finished splitting up the scrap two-by-fours into kindling
I glanced up at the half moon, pink, chill refinery cloud light
Two big black birds flew over, their wings whooshing and low
Two ravens
(But only two)
Their black feathers tinted in the sunset
I knew these birds were omens but of what I wasn't sure
They were flying out toward the island where we hoped to move
You were probably inside
You were probably aching, wanting not to die
Your body transformed
I couldn't bear to look so I turned my head west
Like an early death
Now I can only see you on the fridge in lifeless pictures
And in every dream I have at night
And in every room I walk into like
Here, where I sit the next October, still seeing your eyes, pleading and afraid
Full of love
Calling out from another place
Because you're not here
I watched you die in this room, then I gave your clothes away
I'm sorry, I had to
And now I'll move
I will move with our daughter
We will ride over water
With your ghost underneath the boat
What was you is now burnt bones
And I cannot be at home
I'm running, grief flailing

kitty.lineNext: The taxis run across my feet
stanza: They massacred the buffalo
Kitty corner from the bank
The taxis run across my feet
And my eyes have turned to blanks
In my little box
At the top of the stairs
With my Indian rug
And a pipe to share

kettle.linePrev: I could have been a signpost, could have been a clock
kettle.line: As simple as a kettle, steady as a rock
kettle.lineNext: I could be here and now
stanza: I could have been a sailor, could have been a cook
A real live lover, could have been a book
I could have been a signpost, could have been a clock
As simple as a kettle, steady as a rock

kettle.line: 11 ContributorsSoldier’s Things LyricsDavenports and kettle drums
stanza: 11 ContributorsSoldier’s Things LyricsDavenports and kettle drums
And swallowtail coats
Tablecloths and patent leather shoes
Bathing suits and bowling balls
And clarinets and rings
And all this radio really needs is a fuse
